title: Aluminum Price (Monthly) - Historical Data & Trends
summary: Aluminum price reached 3372.95 USD/mt for March 2026, marking a 10.04% increase from the previous month and 26.88% from one year ago. The data was last updated on April 2, 2026, reflecting ongoing upward trends in the market.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum fell to 3,468.50 USD/T on April 2, 2026, down 1.57% from the previous day, amid fears of supply squeeze due to lack of clear timeline on the Iran war from President Trump. Prices remain near four-year highs, up 5.83% over the past month and 41.22% year-over-year. Forecasts suggest trading at 3372.15 USD/T by quarter-end.

title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Global aluminum prices retreated 1.62% to $3,461.28/ton, with Chinese markets at ¥23,820/ton, due to technical profit-taking. Prices stay elevated near four-year highs from supply shocks like missile strikes on Al Taweelah smelter, low LME inventories, and energy cost surges from Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

title: World's Top Aluminium Smelter Cuts Output Due to Shipping Chaos
summary: The world's largest single-site aluminium smelter in Bahrain, Aluminium Bahrain (Alba), cut its output by about 20% due to shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. This shutdown affects three reduction lines, representing roughly 2.2% of global aluminium production and aims to preserve raw material inventory. The disruption could tighten global supply chains and drive aluminium prices higher.

title: Powering Up American Aluminum: A Roadmap for Next Generation Supply Chain Resilience
summary: A new white paper outlines pathways for U.S. aluminum self-sufficiency, including building new smelters (5-6 years, $4-6 billion each) and restarting idled capacity to address the 4 million metric ton supply gap. Recycled aluminum production hit record levels in 2024, being 95% less energy-intensive, with potential to meet 25-50% of the gap via better scrap collection. Domestic primary production has declined, but opportunities exist for expansion with reliable power.

title: Aluminium Above $3,500, LNG Near $20, and Wheat Planting Hits ...
summary: Aluminium prices are climbing sharply due to direct disruptions to smelting capacity in the UAE and Bahrain, removing over 1 million tons of expected production. Geopolitical shocks in the Strait of Hormuz are impacting exports and logistics for aluminum facilities. This contributes to tightening metals markets amid broader commodity pressures.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices fell to 3103 CNY/T on April 3, 2026, down 0.06% from the previous day amid demand concerns and policy uncertainty. Steel rebar futures dropped below CNY 3,100 per ton, reaching a three-week low. Reports indicate pressure from potential revisions to US tariffs on steel and aluminum, keeping some at near 50% while lowering others to 25%.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel rose to 1,063.94 USD/T on March 31, 2026, up 0.09% from the previous day, marking the highest since January 2024. Over the past month, prices gained 4.72%, and year-over-year, they are up 18.88%. Recent updates show continued increases, with HRC Steel at 996.00 USD/T and gains of 2.8% over four weeks.

title: Steel Production in the United States decreased to 6500 Thousand Tonnes in February
summary: U.S. steel production declined from 7,100 thousand tonnes in January 2026 to 6,500 thousand tonnes in February 2026. This represents a decrease in monthly production output for the country.

title: Domestic Raw Steel Production Reaches 1,803,000 Net Tons
summary: U.S. domestic raw steel production was 1,803,000 net tons in the week ending March 28, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 77.9 percent. This represents a 5.9 percent increase compared to the same week in the previous year and a 1.2 percent increase from the prior week.

title: World Crude Steel Production Declines in January 2026
summary: Global crude steel production for 69 reporting countries reached 147.3 million tonnes in January 2026, representing a 6.5 percent decrease compared to January 2025. This data reflects a significant contraction in worldwide steel production.

title: Baosteel’s Danieli Fastarc Zerobucket EAF Beats Production Record
summary: Danieli announced that Baosteel’s new Fastarc Zerobucket electric arc furnace (EAF) meltshop has beaten the 10-year production record by reaching 31 heats daily. This innovation highlights advancements in efficient and sustainable steelmaking technology. The Zerobucket EAF likely contributes to reduced emissions and improved sustainability in steel production.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Copper futures rose to 5.55 USD/Lbs on March 31, 2026, up 1.30% from the previous day, extending a rebound from a three-month low. Despite a 5.89% monthly decline, prices remain 10.40% higher year-over-year amid pressures from Middle East conflicts and energy costs. Copper held steady near $5.5 per pound on Tuesday, on track for a nearly 10% March loss.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from a major producer. The news highlights ongoing operational challenges in key mining regions.

title: No sign yet of China’s plan to cut copper output in major smelters’ results
summary: Major Chinese smelters are planning to raise or maintain copper output in 2026, despite a public commitment by the state-linked industry association to cut production by over 10%. This indicates no immediate signs of the planned reduction materializing. The development comes amid ongoing market pressures on copper supply.

title: Barrick warns of “significant increases” to budget, timeline for Pakistan copper project Reko Diq
summary: Barrick has warned of significant increases to the budget and timeline for the Reko Diq copper project in Pakistan, one of the world’s largest undeveloped copper deposits. The project is located in the remote Balochistan province. These delays could impact future copper production capacity from the site.

title: Copper: Chile output slump and mine cuts – ING
summary: Chile, a leading global copper producer, is experiencing an output slump along with mine cuts, as noted by ING analysts. This contributes to tightening supply dynamics in the copper market. The developments are influencing copper prices amid broader market trends.

title: Copper-Aluminum Divergence Unveils The Complex Iran War Impact
summary: The divergence between copper and aluminum prices highlights the complex impact of the Iran war on metal markets. Copper production and supply chains may face disruptions due to geopolitical tensions. This analysis reveals ongoing risks to global copper output.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto has produced the first copper cathode using its proprietary Nuton bioleaching technology at Gunnison Copper’s Johnson Camp mine, relying on microorganisms for extraction. The copper achieves a mine-to-metal carbon footprint of 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg, the lowest in the U.S., powered by 100% renewable energy certificates. This breakthrough demonstrates cleaner, faster copper production at industrial scale after just 18 months from concept.

title: Nickel Price Analysis Today
summary: Global nickel prices retreated 0.93% to $17,081.3 per ton, driven by persistent structural oversupply and sluggish downstream demand from the Asian stainless steel sector. Elevated LME inventories, rising Chinese social stocks, and a robust US dollar are further pressuring the metal, though Indonesia's mining quota restrictions and proposed export taxes provide a cost floor.

title: Nickel Futures Trading Analysis
summary: Nickel futures are trading around $17,200, falling from March highs near $17,770. Supply remains constrained as Indonesia advances its proposed export tax on nickel, while disruptions to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz add logistical pressure. Trading Economics forecasts nickel to trade at $17,924.26 by the end of Q2 2026.

title: Nickel Price Today: Indonesia Supply Cuts Drive Market Near 19k
summary: Indonesia has cut its 2026 nickel ore production quotas by approximately 34% compared to last year, targeting 250-260 million tonnes to align with domestic processing capacity and conserve reserves. This supply discipline has supported nickel prices at $18,639.14 USD, up 11.45% year-to-date, with the market reacting positively by reducing surplus fears. Technically, prices are consolidating near a 19-month high, with potential to test $19,000 if momentum holds.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: Indonesia announced a flat-to-down 2026 nickel ore production target of 250-260 million tons, bolstering prices that bounced between $16,500-$18,500/ton after exceeding $19,000. EV demand shows robust 20% underlying growth, with forecasts of 6-7% annual nickel demand increase driven by market expansion. Analysts predict prices reaching $20,000 per ton soon if production discipline is confirmed, highlighting a structural deficit.

title: Mining with integrity to advance sustainable nickel downstreaming
summary: Indonesia's nickel downstreaming policy since 2020 has boosted economic value by processing ore into high-value battery materials. PT Vale is advancing sustainability through emissions cuts of 33% by 2030, carbon intensity reduction up to 50%, and innovations like heat recovery and electrification. Reclamation efforts restore mined areas to near-natural conditions with 90% vegetation success.

title: Brazilian Nickel's Piauí Nickel Metais achieves Nickel Mark sustainability award
summary: Brazilian Nickel Ltd. announced that Piauí Nickel Metals received the Nickel Mark award for its commitment to responsible and sustainable practices across the nickel value chain. This recognizes excellence in sustainability in nickel production. The award highlights ongoing innovations in sustainable mining.

title: Metso awarded repeat order for sustainable tailings filtration technology for Huayou Group’s laterite nickel mine
summary: Metso has received a repeat order to deliver sustainable tailings filtration technology for the Huayou Group’s laterite nickel mine project in Indonesia. This technology supports environmentally friendly nickel processing. It advances sustainability in nickel mining operations.

title: NOVONIX’s patented, all-dry, zero-waste, cathode synthesis technology
summary: NOVONIX has developed a patented all-dry, zero-waste cathode synthesis technology that significantly reduces costs and environmental impact of nickel-based cathode production for batteries. This innovation supports sustainable nickel use in electric vehicles. It was highlighted in recent Nickel Institute commentary.

title: Cogne Acciai Speciali SpA Announces Its Commitment to Sustainability with ResponsibleSteel™ Certification
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali has embarked on the ResponsibleSteel™ certification process for its Aosta site, a global standard promoting sustainability across the steel production value chain. This decision formalizes the company's commitment to environmental, social, and transparency standards as the steel industry faces increasing pressure to meet environmental and social challenges.
